task_objective: Review the latest Steam Deck conflict-at-launch logs and make the README introduction and demo presentation more approachable.
design_decisions: Keep the investigation read-only and cross-check plugin claims against Steam's process, compatibility, and journal logs.
Treat the one-time SIGSTOP process-tree snapshot as insufficient when Steam can add later processes to the app scope.
Prefer freezing the Steam user-systemd app scope in a future fix so processes attached after the initial launch event inherit the gate.
Place the user-provided assets/demo.webp directly below the opening description so readers see the plugin before the feature list.
Rewrite the opening as a user-centered description of the plugin's everyday value instead of an implementation-oriented definition.
Add a cache-buster to the README demo reference for the next release.
results: The plugin log reported PID 12992 paused through conflict selection, but Steam logs proved that PIDs 13305 and 13311 were added later and Wolverine.exe initialized before the user selected a resolution.
Confirmed a launch-gate coverage defect: the current success log means at least one process was signaled, not that every process associated with the Steam app remained stopped.
Verified SteamOS systemd 257 supports user-unit freeze and thaw for a scope-level correction.
Updated the README introduction and added the demo image near the top.
